name: Tofu Schnitzel with Lemon-Caper Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Lemon-Caper Sauce:
3 tablespoons butter
juice of 2 lemons
2 tablespoons capers -- rinsed and drained
1. Slice the tofu into 1/2-inch thick pieces. Coat both sides of each slice with flour.
2. Dip each piece in the beaten eggs (you may mix a little water if desired).
3. Roll the coated slices in crispbread crumbs, ensuring they are evenly covered.
4. Heat the butter and oil in a large frying pan over moderate heat. Cook the tofu on one side until it is crisp and brown, about 10 minutes. Flip and cook the other side for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until both sides are crispy and golden brown. Remove from the pan and place on a serving dish.
5. To make the sauce, melt the butter in the frying pan over low heat. Add the lemon juice and stir to combine. Pour the sauce over the tofu, ensuring it is evenly distributed. Garnish with capers before serving.
